pi_items.source: Task specification and DHIS2 naming conventions for percentage indicators
Task specification and DHIS2 visualization naming conventions
Sierra Leone DHIS2 database contains 'Rapid diagnostic test for Malaria positive' (DE_358918) which matches all keywords
Sierra Leone DHIS2 database contains 'Rapid diagnostic test for Malaria' elements (both positive DE_358918 and negative DE_358919) representing total tested cases
DHIS2 Documentation confirms Percentage indicator type uses factor 100 (https://docs.dhis2.org/en/implement/database-design/aggregate-system-design/indicators.html)

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the official Sierra Leone DHIS2 demo database, which contains real health data from DHIS2 implementation in Sierra Leone. The database includes Rapid Diagnostic Test (RDT) data elements for Malaria (DE_358918: positive tests, DE_358919: negative tests). The task creates a percentage indicator using these elements with a factor of 100, which is the standard DHIS2 configuration for percentage-type indicators. The indicator calculates RDT positivity rate as: (Malaria RDT positive / Malaria RDT tested) × 100.
